design_decisions: Bootstrap generates a new, stack-agnostic minimal payload (verify_adapter.py, lifecycle.py, common.py, generic rule/command/skill stubs) instead of copying this repo's own CCSIF-specific Phase 0-5B scripts verbatim into an unrelated target repo.

scaffold_tree() is strictly additive-only (_write_if_missing never overwrites); rollback-safety follows directly from never overwriting or deleting, so an interrupted run is simply resumed by re-running.
merge_claude_md() refuses to touch CLAUDE.md at all once any '## Source-of-Truth Commands' section already exists, rather than attempting a partial patch that could corrupt a hand-authored block.
bootstrap_local_settings() mirrors phase2_memory.bootstrap_local_settings's documented contract exactly: unchanged/updated/created triad, fail-closed on unreadable JSON, never touches unrelated keys.

detect_stack() never fabricates a shell test/lint command for a docs-only repo; CLAUDE.md's generated Source-of-Truth block gets a 'rubric' placeholder instead, pointing at the adapter's non-code verifier protocol.

Portability proof runs every post-bootstrap step (validate/smoke/status) as an independent fresh subprocess with HOME/USERPROFILE pointed at a directory that does not exist, proving fresh-clone recovery and zero ~/.claude/* dependency empirically rather than by code inspection.

Context-pressure measurement is an explicitly documented, reproducible proxy (always-loaded instruction budget + multi-plan dispatcher-load-vs-naive-inline ratio), not live token metering, per the roadmap's own allowance for a proxy when live measurement is unavailable.

verification_commands.notes: 127 tests total (106 prior + 21 new), no regression
both_passed: true for code-stack-python and non-code-research-pipeline workloads
dispatcher load 15.9% of naive-inline baseline; always-loaded budget 35% of 400-line cap; within_target: true

incidents_caught_during_implementation: taxonomy_check's global-path-dependency scanner flagged four `~/.claude/*` mentions inside bootstrap_control_plane.py's and phase5c_portability_proof.py's own docstrings/comments (documenting the no-dependency guarantee, not creating one); fixed by rewording each to include an explicit negation word ('never'/'must never') within the checker's negation window, re-verified control_plane_check.py and taxonomy_check.py PASS after each fix.
scope_explicitly_excluded: Phase 6: determinism ladder promotions/demotions, adversarial verification battery
Phase 7: distribution, versioning, plugin packaging of the stable core
remaining_risks: Context-pressure figures (15.9%/35%) are a documented byte-based proxy, not live Agent-SDK token metering; no such instrumentation is available in this environment.
The portability proof uses fresh temp-directory copies to simulate a fresh clone; no real `git clone` of a pushed remote was performed (no remote was designated for this task).

Bootstrap's generated framework is a deliberately minimal payload; it does not transplant this repo's own accumulated Phase 0-5B tooling (session harness, subagent tracking, dynamic workflow engine), which remains CCSIF-specific and is Phase 7's concern.

add_smoke_target_to_claude_md()'s fence-detection assumes the bootstrap-generated CLAUDE.md's own ```bash fence style; a differently-formatted pre-existing CLAUDE.md would make the 'smoke' verify target report exit_code 2 (unavailable) rather than silently succeeding -- this is the adapter's correct deterministic behavior for a missing target, not a crash.
Phases 6 and 7 remain unimplemented and are not implicitly validated by these Phase 5C changes.
next_goal: Phase 5C is complete. Do not begin Phase 6 or Phase 7 until explicitly directed.
